does: This paper introduces VEFX-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark system for video editing consisting of two components: VEFX-Dataset, a large-scale human-annotated dataset of 5,049 video editing examples spanning 9 major categories and 32 subcategories with quality labels across three dimensions (Instruction Following, Rendering Quality, Edit Exclusivity), and VEFX-Reward, a specialized reward model for assessing video editing quality. The reward model processes triplets of source video, editing instruction, and edited output to evaluate editing performance, providing an alternative to expensive manual inspection and generic vision-language models.
connects: This work addresses a critical gap in instruction-guided video editing, building on prior research in AI-assisted content creation and video understanding. It extends earlier work on video generation and editing by providing systematic evaluation infrastructure, responding to limitations in existing datasets that lack edited outputs, scale, or human quality annotations. The paper builds on reward modeling approaches used in other vision domains and text-to-video generation, adapting these techniques specifically for the video editing task where evaluating instruction adherence and technical quality simultaneously is essential.
enables: VEFX-Bench establishes a standardized evaluation framework that enables fair comparison of video editing systems and supports development of new editing methods optimized for both instruction following and output quality. The benchmark facilitates training of improved editing models using learned reward signals, opens research directions in multi-dimensional video quality assessment, and provides infrastructure for scaling AI-assisted professional video creation workflows. It also enables exploration of how different editing categories require specialized evaluation criteria and potentially guides development of category-specific editing systems.
